name: Neuronal reprogramming-factor orthologs did not improve worm cognition or lifespan
summary: Toraason 2024 provides a small model-organism limiting result: neuron-specific expression of OSK-factor orthologs did not improve associative memory or lifespan and could impair chemotaxis.
statement: Neuron-specific expression of C. elegans OSK-factor orthologs did not improve short-term associative memory or lifespan and could further disrupt chemotaxis behavior.
population_or_model: Aging C. elegans neurons expressing reprogramming-factor orthologs
time_horizon: Doxycycline-inducible expression during aging with memory, lifespan, and chemotaxis readouts
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports no memory or lifespan improvement and possible chemotaxis disruption without a single effect size.
caveats: C. elegans reprogramming-factor orthologs are distant from mammalian OSK/OSKM therapy.
The result is useful as a generalization boundary, not as decisive evidence against mammalian partial reprogramming.
Neuron-specific expression may not represent systemic or targeted mammalian delivery.
